english: v. rgyúd-pa (rgyud pa) extr. — rtsa-tór (rtsa tor) Sch.: 'lower end and top' (?) (should perh. be rtsa-tóg (rtsa tog)); rtsa-mí (rtsa mi) Tar. 191, 3 is rendered by Schf. with 'Haupt-Mann', principal man. — rtsa-lág (rtsa lag) (Schr.: root and branches) Lex. relations, kindred; rtsa-lag-cán (rtsa lag can) having relations, rtsa-lag-méd (rtsa lag med) without relations Cs. — rtsa-sés (rtsa shes) Sch.: primitive wisdom. — 4. symb. num.: 9. — II. v. rtsa (rtsa) vein. Note. rtsa (rtsa), vein, is traced by Tibetan scholars back to rtsá-ba (rtsa ba), the veins being the 'roots of life'; in a dictionary the words are better treated separately.
= rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) seldom, v. rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) I, 3.

= rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) root, rtsád-nas ɣcód-pa (rtsad nas gcod pa) Mil. to root out, to eradicate; rtsad ɣcód-pa (rtsad gcod pa), = rtsa-brdár ɣcód-pa (rtsa brdar gcod pa), = tsar (tsar) and ɣsar ɣcód-pa (gsar gcod pa), to search, investigate Mil.; gar bźugs rtsad bcád-nas (gar bzhugs rtsad bcad nas) to inquire, search for a person's place of abode Pth.

ferment, barm, yeast, prepared of barley-flour; rtsabs-kúr (rtsabs kur) a sweetish sort of bread, made up with it Ld.; rtsábs-mo (rtsabs mo) a beverage brewed from roasted meal (rtsám-pa (rtsam pa)) and water, and made to ferment by adding butter-milk, esp. liked in winter; also called btság-mo (btsag mo); źó-rtsabs (zho rtsabs) Sch. milk-brandy, not known to us.

I. sbst. 1. roast-flour, flour from roasted grain, ͵bras-rtsam ('bras rtsam) of rice, gro-rtsam (gro rtsam) of wheat, nas-rtsam (nas rtsam) of barley, this last the most common; stirred with water, beer, or tea into a pap, it is the usual food in C. — rtsám-͵bru (rtsam 'bru) roast-flour and grain = victuals in gen. Kun. — rtsám-rin (rtsam rin) the price of flour Sch. — 2. urine Lt. rtsam-mdóg (rtsam mdog) colour of urine. II. vb. v. rtsóm-pa (rtsom pa).

1. skill, dexterity, adroitness, accomplishment; in the first place physical skill, lag-rtsal-cán (lag rtsal can) of a skilful, practised hand W.; sgyu-rtsál (sgyu rtsal) id., stobs daṅ sgyu-rtsál (stobs dang sgyu rtsal) strength and dexterity Glr., skilfulness; rtsal(daṅ) ldán(-pa) (rtsal (dang) ldan (-pa)) skilful, expert, adroit, rtsal-méd (rtsal med) the contrary; rtsal ɣran-pa (rtsal gran pa) to vie in skill, rtsal sbyóṅ-ba (rtsal sbyong ba) to practise, or improve one's self in skill Mil.; rtsal śor (rtsal shor) all skill is gone, rgud (rgud) id. Sch.; stobs-(kyi) rtsal (stobs (kyi) rtsal), Lex. strength, energy, mťu-rtsál (mthu rtsal) and rtsal-mťu (rtsal mthu) prob. id. Dzl., S.g.; rtsal-čé-ba (rtsal che ba) or rtsal-po-čé (rtsal po che) adroit as a gymnastic, wrestler etc.; also sbst. athlete, juggler etc. Dzl.; rtsál-gyi mčóṅs (rtsal gyi mchongs) a gymnastic feat Lex.; rtsal-sbyoṅ (rtsal sbyong) bodily exercise, nimbleness, agility, baṅ-rtsal-sbyoṅ (bang rtsal sbyong) nimbleness in running, ɣśóg-rtsal-sbyoṅ (gshog rtsal sbyong) agility in flying Mil.; čú-rtsal (chu rtsal) feats performed in the water; the art of swimming Pth.; vulgo W. also for natural, innate abilities: míg-rtsal-mkán (mig rtsal mkhan) keen-sighted, míg-rtsal nyams (mig rtsal nyams) of a weak sight; rtsal-tón (rtsal ton) Sch. 'skilful, masterly' (?) — 2. in later times used in a special sense of skill, expertness in contemplation, cf. sgóm-pa (sgom pa); so frq. with Mil.; byaṅ-čub-séms-kyi rtsal ɣsum (byang chub sems kyi rtsal gsum); lam-gag-méd-kyi rtsál-k'a (lam gag med kyi rtsal kha) such accomplishments 'as will clear the road', — ascetical terms familiar only to the initiated.

1. all fluids of a somewhat greater consistency, such as the juice of some fruits, paints, varnish etc., rtsi-cán (rtsi can) viscid, sticky, clammy; *tsi gyág-pa* (*tsi gyag pa*) C., *si gyáb-de* (*si gyab de*), kú-ce (ku ce), táṅ-ce (tang ce) W. to colour, to paint, *tsi táṅ-wa* (*tsi tang wa*) C. also to solder; ldab-pa(?) (ldab pa(?)) Sch. to lacker, to varnish; sbráṅ-rtsi (sbrang rtsi) honey; nád-kyi rkyen rtsi (nad kyi rkyen rtsi) a medical draught, potion Dzl. VL, 7, (another reading: sman (sman)); bdúd-rtsi (bdud rtsi) nectar; tsón-rtsi (tson rtsi) painter's colour, dkár-rtsi (dkar rtsi) white-wash, nág-rtsi (nag rtsi) black paint, dmár-rtsi (dmar rtsi) red paint; *sér-tsi* (*ser tsi*) C. gilding, *ṅúl-tsi* (*ngul tsi*) silvering C. — 2. applied to external appearance: *dóṅ-si* (*dong si*) W. complexion; even spa rtsi ǰam k'a-dóg légs-pa de (spa rtsi jam kha dog legs pa de) this cane, as to its outside smooth, as to colour beautiful Mil. (unless rtsi (rtsi) be = shell, bark, rind?)
